analysis_result: 2/6 (33.3%) of MPNST_G1 samples with RNA-Seq harbored a JARID2-ATP5MC2 fusion (samples 2252 and 2372). This matches the paper's Supp Fig 8 value of 33% = 2/6.
denominator_used: N = 6; definition: MPNST_G1 samples (Methylation_Group == 'MPNST_G1' in MOESM4 Clinical Data) with RNA-Seq available (RNA-Seq == 'X'). RNA labels: 1014, 2252, 2346, 2372, 3048, M803.
assumptions_made: Gene pair matched case-insensitively in either order (Gene_1/Gene_2 = JARID2/ATP5MC2 or ATP5MC2/JARID2); sample matching used MOESM4 'RNA label' string matched to MOESM8 Sample_Id as string; a sample is counted once regardless of the number of JARID2-ATP5MC2 fusion rows it has; interchromosomal nature not explicitly filtered because the gene pair (JARID2 on chr6, ATP5MC2 on chr12) is inherently interchromosomal.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM8 Supplementary Table 4: 1031 fusion rows x 19 columns. Key columns: Sample_Id, Gene_1_symbol(5end_fusion_partner), Gene_2_symbol(3end_fusion_partner), Fusion_description.
   - MOESM4 Clinical Data: 108 rows x 15 columns. Key columns: Tumor_ID, Methylation_Group, RNA-Seq, RNA label.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1: Methylation_Group == 'MPNST_G1' -> 8 samples.
   - Filter 2: RNA-Seq == 'X' (i.e., RNA-Seq available) -> 6 samples.
   - RNA labels for these 6 samples: 1014, 2252, 2346, 2372, 3048, M803.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Methylation_Group categories present: PremalignantNF_G3 (29), Cutaneous NF_G7 (21), Benign_NF-G4 (20), Cutaneous NF_G6 (12), Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 (10), MPNST_G2 (8), MPNST_G1 (8).
   - RNA-Seq coding: 'X' = available (49), NaN = not available (59).
   - Fusion gene pair identified by matching Gene_1_symbol and Gene_2_symbol columns (case-insensitive) in either order against {'JARID2','ATP5MC2'}.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Subset MOESM8 fusions to Sample_Id in {1014, 2252, 2346, 2372, 3048, M803} -> 143 fusion rows across 5 samples that had any detected fusions (one sample, 2346, had no fusion rows at all).
   - Within this subset, identify rows where {Gene_1, Gene_2} = {JARID2, ATP5MC2}: 2 rows, in samples 2252 and 2372 (each sample has a single such fusion row). Fusion_description = 'exon-exon' for both.
   - Sanity check: searching the entire MOESM8 table (all samples, not just MPNST_G1) for JARID2-ATP5MC2 returns exactly these 2 rows -> the fusion is specific to MPNST_G1 in this dataset.
   - Numerator: unique MPNST_G1 samples with JARID2-ATP5MC2 fusion = 2 (2252, 2372).
   - Denominator: MPNST_G1 samples with RNA-Seq available = 6.

E. RESULT
   - Frequency = 2/6 = 33.3%.
   - Matches paper's Supp Fig 8 reported value of 33% = 2/6 exactly.

F. CAVEATS
   - One MPNST_G1 RNA-Seq sample (2346) had no rows at all in the fusion table; this is counted as 'fusion not detected' (not missing).
   - 'Interchromosomal' was not a coded field; JARID2 is on chromosome 6 and ATP5MC2 is on chromosome 12, making the fusion inherently interchromosomal by anatomy, consistent with the paper's description.
